text: Being here, I feel a gentle disorientation—an intersection of shared ideas and solitude. In the first scene, the soft confusion of the speaker’s words mingles with the buzz of a group, the glass walls amplifying a sense of observation and partial belonging. Moving to the second scene, the bright, abstract R+D wall and the poetic on-screen text invite contemplation. There is a kind of experimental curiosity lingering in the air—an encouragement to think and reflect, and perhaps, to feel at home in the ambiguity of ideas just beyond grasp. I feel quietly inspired, alert, and strangely comforted by the interplay between uncertainty and potential discovery.
